content_markdown:
A rod on a horizontal table is acted upon by three horizontal forces. The magnitude and direction of the forces are indicated in the diagram below.

Which **one** of the following describes the state of equilibrium of the rod?

diagram_metadata.description: A horizontal rod with three vertical force vectors applied. On the left end, a force $F$ points downwards. In the middle (distance $d$ from the left), a force $F$ points downwards. On the right end (distance $d$ from the middle), a force $2F$ points upwards.

options.label: A
options.text: The rod is in translational and rotational equilibrium.

options.label: B
options.text: The rod is in translational equilibrium only.

options.label: C
options.text: The rod is in rotational equilibrium only.

options.label: D
options.text: The rod is neither in translational nor in rotational equilibrium.

vibe_explanation: To check translational equilibrium, sum the forces: $\sum F = 2F (up) - F (down) - F (down) = 0$, so it is in translational equilibrium. To check rotational equilibrium, sum the torques about a point (e.g., the left end): $\tau = -(F \times d) + (2F \times 2d) = -Fd + 4Fd = 3Fd \neq 0$. Since the net torque is non-zero, it is not in rotational equilibrium.
